topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara traced the fine print of the award letter again, the paper feeling suddenly abrasive under her fingertips. The words swam, shifting from proud declaration to a hollow, sickening realization. It was hers. Her hard work, her late nights fueled by instant coffee, the sacrifices she’d made—all directed toward a vacant slot. A cold knot tightened in her stomach, a sensation she tried to physically push down with a sharp intake of breath. "No," she muttered to the empty library stacks, her voice tight. She reread the section about the intended recipient, the name unfamiliar yet undeniably real. A wave of fierce indignation washed over her, hot and blinding. She slammed the letter onto the desk, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet study room. She wouldn't accept this. She wouldn't let this slip by.

[story 2]

Mark stared at the acceptance email, his jaw clenched so tightly he could feel the muscle twitching beneath his skin. The numbers didn't compute. He had poured every ounce of his ambition into this application, the sheer force of his dedication evident in every essay. Yet, the funding details pointed to a different applicant, someone whose profile seemed a perfect match for the scholarship's stated criteria, but not for him. He pushed his chair back, the scrape of metal against the floor sounding aggressive. He stood up, pacing the narrow confines of his dorm room. His hands balled into fists at his sides. "This can't be right," he ground out, his voice low and strained. He needed proof, needed to challenge this discrepancy, to force the truth into the open, even if it meant facing an overwhelming wall of bureaucracy.

[story 3]

"I don't understand," Liam said, his voice carefully level, though the tremor in his hands betrayed the internal turmoil. He held the official documentation, his gaze fixed on the discrepancy between his name and the designated recipient. A deep furrow creased his brow, a physical manifestation of the resistance building within him. He felt a sudden, desperate need to argue, to dismantle this arrangement piece by piece until it yielded the correct outcome. He took a slow, deliberate breath, forcing his shoulders back, adopting a posture of unwavering resolve. He wasn't going to let this outcome stand unchallenged; he would pursue every avenue until the allocation was corrected, no matter how tedious the process became.
